thailand_result.summary: You need to file the Thailand Digital Arrival Card (TDAC).
thailand_result.note: All foreign visitors must file the TDAC. It is always free. Anyone charging you is a middleman.

options.label: By air to a major airport (CUN, MEX, GDL, TIJ, PVR, SJD, etc.)
By air to a smaller airport

mexico_air_major.summary: You probably do NOT need a separate FMM-E.
mexico_air_major.note: Most major Mexican airports (CUN, MEX, GDL, TIJ, PVR, SJD) now use a passport stamp instead of a separate FMM. The stamp itself authorizes your stay, no separate filing needed.
mexico_air_major.fallback_label: Read the FMM overview if unsure
mexico_air_major.fallback_warning: If you want extra safety, file the FMM-E anyway (free, takes 10 min). It's never a problem at the border to have one filed.

mexico_need.summary: You need to file the FMM-E (Forma Migratoria Múltiple Electrónica).

forms.name: FMM-E
forms.deadline: Any time before arrival; up to 30 days out

mexico_need.note: Land, sea, and smaller-airport entries still require the FMM-E. Free on the INM site. Keep the stub the border officer hands you until you exit Mexico.

dominican_result.summary: You need to file the Dominican Republic E-Ticket.
dominican_result.note: All arrivals and departures need the E-Ticket. One filing covers both directions of your trip. Free at the .gob.do site.

options.label: Brunei or Thailand passport entering by land
Pure transit at KUL (not clearing immigration)

malaysia_exempt.summary: You are EXEMPT from filing the MDAC.
malaysia_exempt.note: Singapore citizens, Malaysian citizens / PRs, Brunei and Thailand passport holders entering by land, pure airside transit travelers, and certain diplomatic passports do not need the MDAC. Just bring your passport.

malaysia_need.summary: You need to file the Malaysia Digital Arrival Card (MDAC).
malaysia_need.note: Free at the .gov.my site. Officer scans your passport at immigration; the MDAC submission auto-pulls.

options.label: ASEAN citizen (Singapore, Thailand, Malaysia, Indonesia, Philippines, etc.)
Europe (Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Italy, Norway, Russia, Spain, Sweden, UK, Belarus)
Other (US, Canada, Australia, China, India, etc.)

vietnam_visa_exempt.summary: You can enter Vietnam visa-free for tourism (typically 15-45 days, depending on your nationality).
vietnam_visa_exempt.note: No e-visa needed for visa-exempt nationalities. Just bring your passport. Verify your specific stay limit on the official Vietnam Immigration Department page before flying.
vietnam_visa_exempt.fallback_label: Read the e-visa guide for context

vietnam_need_evisa.note: $25 USD is the official government fee. Anyone charging more is a middleman. Multi-entry option ($50) and express ($50 surcharge) are also government tiers. iVisa typically charges $99-179 for the same e-visa.

options.label: US, EU, UK, Canada, Australia, Japan, Korea, Singapore (visa-free since 2024)

indonesia_ecd_only.summary: You need the All Indonesia arrival declaration. No visa required.

forms.name: All Indonesia
forms.agency: Direktorat Jenderal Imigrasi / Bea dan Cukai

indonesia_ecd_only.note: All arrivals need the free arrival declaration. Visa-free nationalities don't need a separate e-VOA. Use the official .go.id portal.

indonesia_both.summary: You need BOTH the All Indonesia arrival declaration and the e-VOA (visa).

forms.name: All Indonesia
forms.agency: Direktorat Jenderal Imigrasi / Bea dan Cukai

indonesia_both.note: Two separate official flows. Don't let middlemen bundle them: the arrival declaration is free and the e-VOA has a real $35 government fee.

options.label: Pure airside transit at Changi (not clearing immigration)
Foreign visitor or Singapore PR returning

singapore_exempt.note: Singapore citizens entering on a Singapore passport, and pure airside transit travelers, are exempt. Just bring your passport.

singapore_need.summary: You need to file the Singapore Arrival Card (SGAC).
singapore_need.note: Free at the .gov.sg site. Includes a brief health declaration. eGate-eligible nationalities scan their passport on arrival; the SGAC submission auto-pulls.

philippines_result.summary: You need to file Philippines eTravel.
philippines_result.note: Free at the .gov.ph site. eTravel is a travel declaration, not a visa.

cambodia_result.summary: You need the Cambodia e-Arrival declaration.
cambodia_result.note: The arrival declaration is free. Visa fees are separate when your trip requires a visa.

japan_result.summary: You can use Visit Japan Web for Japan arrival QR workflows.

forms.name: Visit Japan Web
forms.deadline: Before arrival; paper fallback may exist

japan_result.note: Visit Japan Web is free and separate from visa status. Some airport flows can still use paper forms.

korea_result.summary: You need the Korea e-Arrival Card when the official portal applies to your trip.
korea_result.note: Free at the .go.kr portal. This is separate from K-ETA, visa status, and Q-Code.

usa_passport.label: Is your passport from a Visa Waiver Program country?

usa_esta_result.summary: You need an approved ESTA before traveling under the Visa Waiver Program.

forms.name: ESTA
forms.deadline: No later than 72 hours before departure

usa_esta_result.note: ESTA is a travel authorization, not a visa. Eligible Visa Waiver Program travelers need it for air, sea, and land entry. Approval does not guarantee admission.

usa_visa_result.summary: You may need a U.S. visa instead of ESTA.
usa_visa_result.note: If your passport is not covered by the Visa Waiver Program, or you are not sure you meet every condition, check the U.S. embassy or consulate visa guidance for your passport and trip.
usa_visa_result.fallback_label: Read the ESTA overview and Visa Waiver Program check

options.label: I already have a UK visa or UK immigration status
Passport from an ETA-eligible visa-free country

uk_none_result.summary: You do not need a UK ETA.
uk_none_result.note: British and Irish citizens do not need an ETA. You also do not need one if you already have a UK visa or permission to live, work, or study in the UK.

uk_eta_result.summary: You need an approved UK ETA before traveling.

forms.name: ETA
forms.deadline: Before you travel; allow up to 3 working days

uk_eta_result.note: A UK ETA is a travel authorization, not a visa. It is valid for 2 years or until the linked passport expires and covers multiple journeys. Every traveler, including a baby or child, needs a separate application.

uk_visa_result.summary: You may need a UK visa instead of an ETA.
uk_visa_result.note: If your passport is not ETA-eligible, or you are not sure you meet the ETA conditions, check the official UK visa guidance for your nationality and trip.
uk_visa_result.fallback_label: Read the UK ETA overview and eligibility check

options.label: By air, including connecting through a Canadian airport
By car, bus, train, or cruise ship
U.S. citizen or U.S. lawful permanent resident

canada_eta_result.summary: You need an approved Canada eTA before flying or connecting through a Canadian airport.

forms.name: eTA
forms.deadline: Before you book or board your flight

canada_eta_result.note: A Canada eTA is valid for up to 5 years or until the linked passport expires, whichever comes first. It supports multiple entries and does not guarantee admission.

canada_none_result.summary: You do not need a Canada eTA for this trip.
canada_none_result.note: Travelers arriving by car, bus, train, or cruise ship normally do not need an eTA. U.S. citizens are exempt. U.S. lawful permanent residents have also been exempt since April 26, 2022; when flying, carry a valid passport plus a valid green card or other proof of U.S. status accepted by IRCC.

canada_visa_result.summary: You may need a Canadian visitor visa instead of an eTA.
canada_visa_result.note: If your passport is visa-required, or you are not sure you meet the eTA conditions, check the official Canada.ca eligibility guidance for your passport, travel history, and route.
canada_visa_result.fallback_label: Read the Canada eTA overview and eligibility check
